title: Polycarpon niveum
intro: Polycarpon niveum, also known as Snowwort, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Polycarpon niveum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Polycarpon niveum is commonly prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create infusions and salves for topical application.
taxonomy: Polycarpon niveum belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Polycarpon. The plant is native to Europe and Western Asia. Polycarpon niveum is classified as a annual herb. It is also known as Snowwort due to its white, woolly stems and leaves.
